title: Living in Boulder vlog 7-2-2017: Roman Buildings Book

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs from Boulder on July 2, 2017, sharing that he's been dealing with breathing problems at night and feeling unwell, prompting plans to change his health routine. He describes his current project of cataloging ancient Roman buildings from a book and mapping them in the context of Boulder, and briefly mentions a bike ride he took with his mom.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Feels toxic and sick; recognizes he needs to make changes
Finds ancient Roman architecture fascinating; excited about building a historical map
Enthusiastic about the trail network and lack of car traffic

entities: Platner and Ashbury (book on buildings of Rome)

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder V-Log. It's 10.03pm, July 2nd. So this is the V-Log for July 2nd, 2017.
I didn't do one yesterday because I was working until I just got passed out, so...
And last night was my second night, and I think, at least second, maybe third, but yeah, at least second, where I couldn't breathe.
So, well, I'm just laying there, like, trying to breathe, and it's hell, so I need to do something, switch up my health.
Health is starting to dip. Plus, I feel really sick, like toxic, so I think now is a good time for me to switch gears
And do the writing inside of the rollers while I trace cobblestones on rum, and then also do 120,000 people.
I already did 40,000 people to make 160,000 people to make my, you know, armies and stuff right here in Boulder.
So, I've got some work to do tomorrow, so I think I'll do that.
Might not start the biking tomorrow.
But on Tuesday, Tuesday, Wednesday, or Thursday, maybe Thursday.
But yeah, then I'll get going for sure, as I have it.
Because I'm also kind of burning out on this book, this book, Platner and Ashby.
Today, for the last two days, I've just been listing all of the, gathering all the houses, I don't know how many there are yet,
But I'm from the list of buildings of Rome and putting them on their place, like the location.
And then later on, I'll build the map.
So, today I was looking at Rome and just studying more about the city of Rome and geography.
And so, right now, I'm looking at it on Google Earth, looking at a lot of, finally, going in and just looking at every single one of the ancient buildings,
Which is super fascinating.
We lost a lot, but thank God we still have a lot of cool stuff to look at.
You know, 2,000-year-old buildings.
There's this one building that it was, they don't even know when it was built, but it was like 300s or something.
And the dome only collapsed in 1826.
And a lot of the destruction was done in the 1500s, in the Middle Ages, as popes.
So, that's weird.
So, yeah, and so that's what I was listening to, the Platner and Ashby, Buildings of Rome Book,
And then, yeah, different Wikipedia articles of all this different stuff.
And also, the population of the city, because then I was also getting super excited about building the map of the area as well,
And the kingdom size and population for different times.
So, I can plug that into Boulder, Boulder Rome, because, yeah, I want to do that, too.
So, yeah, I think that's what I'll do.
Switch gears.
Switch gears.
This week, maybe, on Thursday.
Oh, so, yesterday, let's see, I didn't, day for yesterday, I rode with my mom.
Yesterday I didn't, because she was tired.
But today, we did go on a bike ride.
We went down the bike path towards the east, past the lake, and then the baseball fields, and then back.
And, yeah, so it was fun.
It's just getting strong every day.
And then, so I'd like to maybe day after tomorrow, give her a day to rest day after tomorrow,
Go on another adventure.
Because there's so many trails you can go all over here without having to worry about cars.
Just follow the bike paths.
And it's getting better, too.
Building a new one right up here, so.
Okay, so that's enough talking.
Peace out.
